judgment.summary: Walmart shows strong returns and cash flow but faces challenges with declining earnings growth and a high forward P/E ratio.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimate by 1.8%; next earnings date is 2026-05-21.
positives: Return on equity 21.8% — indicates efficient use of shareholder equity.
Free cash flow $10.55B TTM — strong cash generation ability.
Consensus 'strong buy' from 41 analysts with a mean target of $137.78.
Revenue growth 5.6% YoY — steady top-line expansion.
negatives: Earnings growth -19.0% YoY and quarterly earnings growth -19.4% — significant decline.
Forward P/E 36.90x and PEG ratio 4.88 — valuation appears high relative to earnings growth.
Current ratio 0.79 and Quick ratio 0.20 — liquidity is a concern.
Total debt $68.43B vs Total cash $10.73B — substantial leverage.

news.paragraph: Walmart Inc. (WMT) is reportedly backed by Wall Street analysts and notable investor Louis Navellier. This news comes just ahead of its next earnings report, which is scheduled for May 21, 2026. The market's sentiment appears to be leaning positive, with a consensus of 'strong buy' from analysts.

headlines.title: Walmart Inc. (WMT) Backed by Wall Street and Louis Navellier

examples.body: $WMT Starting a position, been watching since March. Calls or holding shares until next earnings/Q3/Q4
